figures.caption: Figure 1: Overview of the Instruction–Evidence Contrastive Dual-Stream (IECD2) framework

Figure 3: Comparison of created descriptions by IFCD Wang et al. [2025a] and IECD2 on LlavaBench (Liu et al. [2023]) dataset. Text in blue indicates content that is not grounded in the image (i.e., hallucinated or unsupported by the image). 12

Table 5: POPE and AMBER (Discriminative Task) results across VLMs. Best score per metric per model is shown in bold and second best is underlined. Overall, IECD2 consistently delivers strong performance across POPE and AMBER (Discriminative Task), demonstrating an effective balance between instruction following and visual grounding.

Table 6: Hallucination and grounding evaluation across VQA and captioning on VQAv2, MSCOCO, and AMBER (Generative Task). Best per metric is bold, second best is underlined. IECD2 consistently produces superior results to prior methods in hallucination reduction while preserving strong task accuracy across benchmarks.

Figure 2: Token probability distributions of the Instruction stream, Evidence stream and IECD2, with their image caption. IECD2 retains the visual grounding of evidence stream. Image from AMBER (Wang et al. [2024a]) dataset.
